generatedSentence: Nighthawk, a novice gamer, often found himself babysitting his nephew while juggling virtual reality joypads, his psychokinesis subtly at play underneath the rumble of ancient systems. He'd jaywalk through the digital streets, munching a hotdog and a kale pickle, sometimes braking mid-air as a virtual tram passed, knowing longevity in his fictional world required constant maintenance without any bad judgment. He was chuffed by a decent score, but still undecided about his true calling, often finding an anchovy pizza the necessary fuel for his academic pursuits, even though it meant skipping outdoor jogging and instead relied on a virtual handout. One season, a terrible aberration of globalization began to amplify bad virtual voltage, turning innocent juniors into huffy shadow wolves. Nighthawk, sensing the spirit of Roman imperialism trying to deconstruct his beloved habitat, knew he had to act. He had waited and watched, recognizing the pattern as a cruel mimicry of old Nickelodeons, now infecting the youth with a flulike lethargy, making them an easy mark for the coming darkness, which felt unjustifiable and dangerous. The dark forces, backed by obscure academia and hawkers of illicit encryption, sought to skyjack the minds of the masses, moving them like caterpillars towards a lava pit of despair. Nighthawk, now in full gear and helmet, demonstrated precision kickboxing, delivering a powerful uppercut to a giant dragon construct that had emerged from the digital chimneys, its knocker-like head ejecting sharp daggers. This intimate, cinematic moment, entertains no one, as the omega of their world was at stake. With a succinct burst of healing energy, Nighthawk whisked away the hurt and the draugh from the digital paradise, watching as the corrupt loader, a monument to objectifying power, finally dissolved into a circle of light. The virtual jailbreak was complete; the grinders of malevolence silenced. There was no next interview, no need for tandem traveling or hitchhiking – only peace. He had, at anytime, understood the mathematics of this enchanted conflict, ensuring paradise for his world, without any further pain.

imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A lone figure, 'Nighthawk,' wearing a sleek silver chrome helmet with glowing red visor and a form-fitting dark blue armored suit with subtle circuit patterns. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Nighthawk, seen from a medium shot, levitating slightly above a glowing virtual tram track, munching a hotdog, his eyes focused on a holographic UI. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: Nighthawk, a wide shot, stands resolute against a backdrop of distorted, glitching skyscrapers, facing off against a huffy, shadowy wolf-like creature. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: Nighthawk, dynamic low-angle shot, mid-kickboxing uppercut against a colossal, glowing digital dragon with a daggers-ejecting head, amidst falling lava fragments. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: Nighthawk, a close-up, extends a glowing hand towards a group of small, healing, relieved figures, with a vibrant, restored digital paradise city in the background.

The twenty-fourth letter of the Classical and the Modern Greek alphabet, and the twenty-eighth letter of the Old and the Ancient Greek alphabet, i.e. the last letter of every Greek alphabet. Uppercase version: Ω; lowercase: ω.
(often capitalized) The end; the final, last or ultimate in a sequence.
Angular velocity; symbol: ω.
An omega male.
The percentage change in an option value divided by the percentage change in the underlying asset's price.

In omegaverse fiction, a person of a sexually-submissive (and sometimes secondary) gender/sex that is driven by biology, magic, or other means to bond with an alpha, with males of this type often being able to get pregnant.
To amuse (someone); to engage the attention of agreeably.
definitions.example: The motivational speaker not only instructed but also entertained the audience.

definitions.definition: To have someone over at one's home for a party or visit.
definitions.example: They enjoy entertaining a lot.

definitions.definition: To receive and take into consideration; to have a thought in mind.
definitions.example: The committee would like to entertain the idea of reducing the budget figures.

definitions.definition: To take or keep in one's service; to maintain; to support; to harbour; to keep.
To meet or encounter, as an enemy.
To lead on; to bring along; to introduce.
The molten rock ejected by a volcano from its crater or fissured sides.
Magma.
A shade of red, named after the volcanic lava.
The one that follows after this one.
definitions.example: Next, please, don't hold up the queue!

definitions.definition: Nearest in place or position, having nothing similar intervening; adjoining.
Nearest in order, succession, or rank; immediately following (or sometimes preceding) in order.
definitions.example: the next chapter; the next man I see; the next week; the Sunday next before Easter

definitions.definition: Following in a hypothetical sequence of some kind.
definitions.example: The man was driven by his love for money and his desire to become the next Bill Gates.

definitions.definition: Nearest in relationship. (See also next of kin.)
In a time, place, rank or sequence closest or following.
definitions.example: It's the next best thing to ice cream.

definitions.definition: On the first subsequent occasion.
definitions.example: Financial panic, earthquakes, oil spills, riots. What comes next?

definitions.definition: On the side of; nearest or adjacent to; next to.
(usually with the definite article) A place to do the "necessary" business of urination and defecation: an outhouse or lavatory.
Required, essential, whether logically inescapable or needed in order to achieve a desired result or avoid some penalty.
definitions.example: Although I wished to think that all was false, it was yet necessary that I, who thus thought, must in some sense exist.

definitions.definition: Unavoidable, inevitable.
definitions.example: If it is absolutely necessary to use public computers, you should plan ahead and forward your e-mail to a temporary, disposable account.

definitions.definition: Determined, involuntary: acting from compulsion rather than free will.
To move something with quick light sweeping motions.
definitions.example: Vernon whisked the sawdust from his workbench.

definitions.definition: In cooking, to whip e.g. eggs or cream.
definitions.example: The chef prepared to whisk the egg whites for the angel's food cake.

definitions.definition: To move something rapidly and with no warning.
definitions.example: The governess whisked the children from the room before they could see their presents.

definitions.definition: To move lightly and nimbly.
definitions.example: The children whisked down the road to the fair, laughing and chattering as they went.

definitions.definition: A legendary serpentine or reptilian creature.
An animal of various species that resemble a dragon in appearance:
(with definite article, often capitalized) The constellation Draco.
A fierce and unpleasant woman; a harridan.
definitions.example: She’s a bit of a dragon.

definitions.definition: (with definite article, often capitalized) The (historical) Chinese empire or the People's Republic of China.
definitions.example: Napoleon already warned of the awakening of the Dragon.

definitions.definition: Something very formidable or dangerous.
A type of playing-tile (red dragon, green dragon, white dragon) in the game of mahjong.
A luminous exhalation from marshy ground, seeming to move through the air like a winged serpent.
A short musket hooked to a swivel attached to a soldier's belt; so called from a representation of a dragon's head at the muzzle.
A background process similar to a daemon.
A variety of carrier pigeon.
To change place or posture; to go, in any manner, from one place or position to another.
definitions.example: A ship moves rapidly.

definitions.definition: To act; to take action; to begin to act
definitions.example: Come on guys, let's move: there's work to do!

definitions.definition: To change residence, for example from one house, town, or state, to another; to go and live at another place. See also move out and move in.
definitions.example: I decided to move to the country for a more peaceful life.

definitions.definition: (and other games) To change the place of a piece in accordance with the rules of the game.
definitions.example: My opponent's counter was moving much quicker round the board than mine.

definitions.definition: To cause to change place or posture in any manner; to set in motion; to carry, convey, draw, or push from one place to another
definitions.example: The horse moves a carriage.

definitions.definition: To transfer (a piece or man) from one space or position to another, according to the rules of the game
definitions.example: She moved the queen closer to the centre of the board.

definitions.definition: To excite to action by the presentation of motives; to rouse by representation, persuasion, or appeal; to influence.
definitions.example: This song moves me to dance.

definitions.definition: To arouse the feelings or passions of; especially, to excite to tenderness or compassion, to excite (for example, an emotion).
definitions.example: That book really moved me.

definitions.definition: To propose; to recommend; specifically, to propose formally for consideration and determination, in a deliberative assembly; to submit
definitions.example: I move to repeal the rule regarding obligatory school uniform.

definitions.definition: To mention; to raise (a question); to suggest (a course of action); to lodge (a complaint).
To incite, urge (someone to do something); to solicit (someone for or of an issue); to make a proposal to.
To apply to, as for aid.
To request an action from the court.
definitions.example: An attorney moved the court to issue a restraining order.

definitions.definition: To bow or salute upon meeting.
To sell, to market (especially, but not exclusively, illegal products)
The relocation of goods
A causing of a movement
definitions.example: The rats' movings are willed movements.

definitions.definition: That moves or move.
definitions.example: moving pictures

definitions.definition: That causes someone to feel emotion.
Equipment or paraphernalia, especially that used for an athletic endeavor.
Clothing; garments.
Goods; property; household items.
A wheel with grooves (teeth) engraved on the outer circumference, such that two such devices can interlock and convey motion from one to the other; a gear wheel.
A particular combination or choice of interlocking gears, such that a particular gear ratio is achieved.
A configuration of the transmission of a motor car so as to achieve a particular ratio of engine to axle torque.
Recreational drugs, including steroids.
Stuff.
Business matters; affairs; concern.
Anything worthless; nonsense; rubbish.
To provide with gearing; to fit with gears in order to achieve a desired gear ratio.
To be in, or come into, gear.
To dress; to put gear on; to harness.
(usually with to or toward(s)) To design or devise (something) so as to be suitable (for a particular type of person or a particular purpose).
definitions.example: They have geared the hotel mainly at tourists.

definitions.definition: To borrow money in order to invest it in assets.
Great or fantastic
Knickers.
Lycra pants (usually short) used by cyclists.
A worksheet, leaflet, or pamphlet that is given out (usually by hand) for a certain use.
A gift to the poor or needy.
definitions.example: It's a place to get a hand up, not a handout.

definitions.definition: A gift, something obtained without effort.
A two-dimensional geometric figure, a line, consisting of the set of all those points in a plane that are equally distant from a given point (center).
definitions.example: The set of all points (x, y) such that (x − 1)2 + y2 = r2 is a circle of radius r around the point (1, 0).

definitions.definition: A two-dimensional geometric figure, a disk, consisting of the set of all those points of a plane at a distance less than or equal to a fixed distance (radius) from a given point.
Any shape, curve or arrangement of objects that approximates to or resembles the geometric figures.
definitions.example: Children, please join hands and form a circle.

definitions.definition: A specific group of persons; especially one who shares a common interest.
definitions.example: circle of friends

definitions.definition: The orbit of an astronomical body.
A line comprising two semicircles of 30 yards radius centred on the wickets joined by straight lines parallel to the pitch used to enforce field restrictions in a one-day match.
A ritual circle that is cast three times deosil and closes three times widdershins either in the air with a wand or literally with stones or other items used for worship.
A traffic circle or roundabout.
Compass; circuit; enclosure.

An instrument of observation, whose graduated limb consists of an entire circle. When fixed to a wall in an observatory, it is called a mural circle; when mounted with a telescope on an axis and in Y's, in the plane of the meridian, a meridian or transit circle; when involving the principle of reflection, like the sextant, a reflecting circle; and when that of repeating an angle several times continuously along the graduated limb, a repeating circle.
A series ending where it begins, and repeating itself.
A form of argument in which two or more unproved statements are used to prove each other; inconclusive reasoning.
Indirect form of words; circumlocution.
A territorial division or district.
definitions.example: The ten Circles of the Holy Roman Empire were those principalities or provinces which had seats in the German Diet.

definitions.definition: (in the plural) A bagginess of the skin below the eyes from lack of sleep.
definitions.example: After working all night, she had circles under her eyes.

definitions.definition: To travel around along a curved path.
definitions.example: The wolves circled the herd of deer.

definitions.definition: To surround.
definitions.example: A high fence circles the enclosure.

definitions.definition: To place or mark a circle around.
definitions.example: Circle the jobs that you are interested in applying for.

definitions.definition: To travel in circles.
definitions.example: Vultures circled overhead.

definitions.definition: The difference in electrostatic potential between two points in space, especially between live and neutral conductors or the earth.
definitions.example: Be careful when opening high-voltage equipment.

definitions.definition: A negating expression; an answer that shows disagreement or disapproval
A vote not in favor, or opposing a proposition
definitions.example: The workers voted on whether to strike, and there were thirty "yeses" and two "nos".

definitions.definition: (now only used with different, with comparatives more and less, and informally with certain other adjectives such as good and fun) Not, not at all.
definitions.example: I just want to find out whether she's coming or no. (Scotland)

definitions.definition: Without
Like
A nightjar, especially Caprimulgus europaeus.
A New World nightjar of the genus Chordeiles, especially Chordeiles minor.
A person whose preference or custom is to remain awake and active during the night and the early morning hours.
A nighthawker.
(Manchester) An outer garment that covers the body from the waist downwards, covering each leg separately, usually as far as the ankles; trousers.
An undergarment that covers the genitals and often the buttocks and the neighbouring parts of the body; underpants.
Rubbish; something worthless.
definitions.example: The film was a load [or pile] of pants.

definitions.definition: Of inferior quality, rubbish.
definitions.example: Your mobile is pants — why don’t you get one like mine?

definitions.definition: The place where sanctified souls are believed to live after death.
definitions.example: Living in paradise comes with a price.

definitions.definition: (Abrahamic religions) A garden where Adam and Eve first lived after being created.
A very pleasant place; a place full of lush vegetation.
definitions.example: an island paradise in the Caribbean; a shoppers’ paradise

definitions.definition: A very pleasant experience.
An open space within a monastery or adjoining a church, such as the space within a cloister, the open court before a basilica, etc.
A churchyard or cemetery.
The upper gallery in a theatre.
To place (as) in paradise.
To transform into a paradise.
To affect or exalt with visions of happiness.
A younger person.
definitions.example: four years his junior

definitions.definition: A name suffix used after a son's name when his father has the same name (abbreviations: Jnr., Jr., Jun.).
A third-year student at a high school or university.
A junior barrister.
The soul of a person or other creature. What moves through experience into self-definition as souls purpose.
A supernatural being, often but not exclusively without physical form; ghost, fairy, angel.
definitions.example: A wandering spirit haunts the island.

definitions.definition: Enthusiasm.
definitions.example: School spirit is at an all-time high.

definitions.definition: The manner or style of something.
definitions.example: In the spirit of forgiveness, we didn't press charges.

definitions.definition: (usually in the plural) A volatile liquid, such as alcohol. The plural form spirits is a generic term for distilled alcoholic beverages.
Energy; ardour.
One who is vivacious or lively; one who evinces great activity or peculiar characteristics of mind or temper.
definitions.example: a ruling spirit; a schismatic spirit

definitions.definition: Temper or disposition of mind; mental condition or disposition; intellectual or moral state; often in the plural.
definitions.example: to be cheerful, or in good spirits; to be down-hearted, or in bad spirits

definitions.definition: Air set in motion by breathing; breath; hence, sometimes, life itself.
A rough breathing; an aspirate, such as the letter h; also, a mark denoting aspiration.
Intent; real meaning; opposed to the letter, or formal statement.
definitions.example: the spirit of an enterprise, or of a document

definitions.definition: Any of the four substances: sulphur, sal ammoniac, quicksilver, and arsenic (or, according to some, orpiment).
(dyeing) Stannic chloride.
To carry off, especially in haste, secrecy, or mystery.
To animate with vigor; to excite; to encourage; to inspirit; sometimes followed by up.
definitions.example: Civil dissensions often spirit the ambition of private men.

definitions.definition: An abstract representational system used in the study of numbers, shapes, structure, change and the relationships between these concepts.
A person's ability to count, calculate, and use different systems of mathematics at differing levels.
definitions.example: My mathematics is always improving.

definitions.definition: A person who plays any kind of game.
A person whose hobby is video games.
A person who games the system.
(obsolete) A gambler
Any of the seats in the rear of a vehicle.
(especially in the expression take a back seat) A lesser or inferior position; a position of deliberate noninvolvement, in which decision-making or leadership is left to others.
Error, mistake.
An item (or kind of item) of merchandise with negative value; an unwanted good.
Unfavorable; negative; not good.
definitions.example: Abducting children is bad for mothers.

definitions.definition: Not suitable or fitting.
definitions.example: Do you think it is a bad idea to confront him directly?

definitions.definition: Not appropriate, of manners etc.
definitions.example: It is bad manners to talk with your mouth full.

definitions.definition: Unhealthy; liable to cause health problems.
definitions.example: Lard is bad for you. Smoking is bad for you, too. Grapes are bad for dogs but not for humans.

definitions.definition: Sickly, unhealthy, unwell.
definitions.example: I went to the hospital to see how my grandfather was doing. Unfortunately, he's in a bad state.

definitions.definition: Not behaving; behaving badly; misbehaving; mischievous or disobedient.
definitions.example: Stop being bad, or you will get a spanking!

definitions.definition: Tricky; stressful; unpleasant.
definitions.example: Divorce is usually a bad experience for everybody involved.

definitions.definition: Evil; wicked.
definitions.example: Be careful. There are bad people in the world.

definitions.definition: Faulty; not functional.
definitions.example: I had a bad headlight.

definitions.definition: (of food) Spoiled, rotten, overripe.
definitions.example: These apples have gone bad.

definitions.definition: (of breath) Malodorous; foul.
definitions.example: Bad breath is not pleasant for anyone.

definitions.definition: False; counterfeit; illegitimate.
definitions.example: They were caught trying to pass bad coinage.

definitions.definition: Unskilled; of limited ability; not good.
definitions.example: He's a bad gardener; everything he tries to grow ends up dying.

definitions.definition: Of poor physical appearance.
definitions.example: I don't look bad in this dress, do I?

definitions.definition: Bold and daring.
Good, superlative, excellent, cool.
definitions.example: Man, that new car you bought is bad!

definitions.definition: (of a need, want, or pain) Severe, urgent.
definitions.example: He is in bad need of a haircut.

definitions.definition: Overly promiscuous, licentious.
(of a draft/check) Not covered by funds on account.
definitions.example: He gave me a bad check.

definitions.definition: Badly.
definitions.example: I didn't do too bad in the last exam.

definitions.definition: Conditions suitable for an organism or population of organisms to live.
definitions.example: This park offers important amphibian habitat and breeding area.

definitions.definition: A place or type of site where an organism or population naturally occurs.
A terrestrial or aquatic area distinguished by geographic, abiotic and biotic features, whether entirely natural or semi-natural.
A place in which a person lives.
An emotional or psychological humiliation or bad experience.
definitions.example: how to overcome old hurts of the past

definitions.definition: A bodily injury causing pain; a wound or bruise.
Injury; damage; detriment; harm
A roundel azure (blue circular spot).
A band on a trip-hammer helve, bearing the trunnions.
A husk.
To be painful.
definitions.example: Does your leg still hurt? / It is starting to feel better.

definitions.definition: To cause (a creature) physical pain and/or injury.
definitions.example: If anybody hurts my little brother, I will get upset.

definitions.definition: To cause (somebody) emotional pain.
To undermine, impede, or damage.
definitions.example: This latest gaffe hurts the legislator’s reelection prospects still further.

definitions.definition: Wounded, physically injured.
Pained.
A protective head covering, usually part of armour.
That which resembles a helmet in form, position, etc.
To cover with, or as if with, a helmet.
One of the main three types used for the Latin alphabet (the others being italics and blackletter), in which the ascenders are mostly straight.
A novel.
(of type) upright, as opposed to italic
(of text) of or related to the Latin alphabet
A virtual member function of a class.
In effect or essence, if not in fact or reality; imitated, simulated.
definitions.example: In fact a defeat on the battlefield, Tet was a virtual victory for the North, owing to its effect on public opinion.

definitions.definition: Having the power of acting or of invisible efficacy without the agency of the material or measurable part; potential.
Nearly, almost. (A relatively recent development in meaning)
definitions.example: The angry peasants were a virtual army as they attacked the castle.

definitions.definition: Simulated in a computer or online.
definitions.example: The virtual world of his computer game allowed character interaction.

definitions.definition: Operating by computer or in cyberspace; not physically present.
definitions.example: a virtual assistant; a virtual personal trainer

definitions.definition: (of a class member) Capable of being overridden with a different implementation in a subclass.
Pertaining to particles in temporary existence due to the Heisenberg uncertainty principle.
A swinging blow aimed upwards at the opponent's chin.
A cut shot that sends the ball over the wicket-keeper's head.
To strike with an uppercut
The action of the verb to jog.
definitions.example: His jogging of my memory helped me recall what happened that day.

definitions.definition: The practice of running at a relatively slow pace for exercise.
Brief and to the point
Compressed into a tiny area.
definitions.example: Unlike general lossless data compression algorithms, succinct data structures retain the ability to use them in-place, without decompressing them first.

definitions.definition: Wrapped by, or as if by a girdle; closely fitting, wound or wrapped or drawn up tightly.
A dark image projected onto a surface where light (or other radiation) is blocked by the shade of an object.
definitions.example: My shadow lengthened as the sun began to set.

definitions.definition: Relative darkness, especially as caused by the interruption of light; gloom, obscurity.
definitions.example: I immediately jumped into shadow as I saw them approach.

definitions.definition: A area protected by an obstacle (likened to an object blocking out sunlight).
definitions.example: The mountains block the passage of rain-producing weather systems and cast a "shadow" of dryness behind them.

definitions.definition: A reflected image, as in a mirror or in water.
That which looms as though a shadow.
definitions.example: I don't have a shadow of doubt in my mind that my plan will succeed. The shadow of fear of my being outed always affects how I live my life. I lived in her shadow my whole life.

definitions.definition: A small degree; a shade.
definitions.example: He did not give even a shadow of respect to the professor.

definitions.definition: An imperfect and faint representation.
definitions.example: He came back from war the shadow of a man.

definitions.definition: (police) A trainee, assigned to work with an experienced officer.
One who secretly or furtively follows another.
definitions.example: The constable was promoted to working as a shadow for the Royals.

definitions.definition: An inseparable companion.
A drop shadow effect applied to lettering in word processors etc.
An influence, especially a pervasive or a negative one.
A spirit; a ghost; a shade.
(Latinism) An uninvited guest accompanying one who was invited.
In Jungian psychology, an unconscious aspect of the personality.
To shade, cloud or darken.
definitions.example: The artist chose to shadow this corner of the painting.

definitions.definition: To block light or radio transmission from.
definitions.example: Looks like that cloud's going to shadow us.

definitions.definition: To secretly or discreetly track or follow another, to keep under surveillance.
To represent faintly and imperfectly.
To hide; to conceal.
To accompany (a professional) during the working day, so as to learn about an occupation one intends to take up.
To make (an identifier, usually a variable) inaccessible by declaring another of the same name within the scope of the first.
To apply the shadowing process to (the contents of ROM).
Unofficial, informal, unauthorized, but acting as though it were.
definitions.example: The human resources department has a shadow information technology group without headquarters knowledge.

definitions.definition: Having power or influence, but not widely known or recognized.
definitions.example: The director has been giving shadow leadership to the other group's project to ensure its success.

definitions.definition: Acting in a leadership role before being formally recognized.
definitions.example: The insurgents’ shadow government is being crippled by the federal military strikes.

definitions.definition: Part of, or related to, the opposition in government.
A current of air, usually coming into a room or vehicle.
Draw through a flue of gasses (smoke) resulting from a combustion process.
An act of drinking.
The quantity of liquid (such as water, alcohol, or medicine) drunk in one swallow.
definitions.example: She took a deep draft from the bottle of water.

definitions.definition: A dose (of medicine, alcohol, etc.)
Beer drawn from a cask or keg rather than a bottle or can.
Depth of water needed to float a ship; depth below the water line to the bottom of a vessel's hull; depth of water drawn by a vessel.
An early version of a written work (such as a book or e-mail) or drawing.
definitions.example: His first drafts were better than most authors' final products.

definitions.definition: A preliminary sketch or outline for a plan.
A cheque, an order for money to be paid.
Conscription, the system of forcing people to serve in the military.
definitions.example: He left the country to avoid the draft.

definitions.definition: A system of forcing or convincing people to take an elected position.
A system of assigning rookie players to professional sports teams.
The pulling force (tension) on couplers and draft gear during a slack stretched condition.
The bevel given to the pattern for a casting, so that it can be drawn from the sand without damaging the mould.
(possibly obsolete) The action or an act (especially of a beast of burden or vehicle) of pulling something along or back.
definitions.example: using oxen for draft   shot forth an arrow with a mighty draft

definitions.definition: (possibly obsolete) The act of drawing in a net for fish.
(possibly obsolete) That which is drawn in; a catch, a haul.
definitions.example: he cast his net, which brought him a very great draft

definitions.definition: A quantity that is requisitioned or drawn out from a larger population.
To write a first version, make a preliminary sketch.
To draw in outline; to make a draught, sketch, or plan of, as in architectural and mechanical drawing.
To write a law.
To conscript a person, force a person to serve in some capacity, especially in the military.
definitions.example: He was drafted during the Vietnam War.

definitions.definition: To select someone (or something) for a particular role or purpose.
definitions.example: There was a campaign to draft Smith to run for President.

definitions.definition: To select and separate an animal or animals from a group.
definitions.example: The calves were drafted from the cows.

definitions.definition: To select a rookie player onto a professional sports team.
definitions.example: After his last year of college football, he was drafted by the Miami Dolphins.

definitions.definition: To follow very closely (behind another vehicle), thereby providing an aerodynamic advantage to both lead and follower and conserving energy or increasing speed.
To draw out; to call forth.
To draw fibers out of a clump, for spinning in the production of yarn.
A checker: a game piece used in the game of draughts.
Ale: a type of beer brewed using top-fermenting yeast.
A mild vesicatory.
An outhouse: an outbuilding used as a lavatory.
Any picture or drawing.
A sudden attack upon an enemy.
To go in the reverse direction.
definitions.example: the train backed into the station;  the horse refuses to back

definitions.definition: To support.
definitions.example: I back you all the way;  which horse are you backing in this race?

definitions.definition: (of the wind) To change direction contrary to the normal pattern; that is, to shift anticlockwise in the northern hemisphere, or clockwise in the southern hemisphere.
(of a square sail) To brace the yards so that the wind presses on the front of the sail, to slow the ship.
(of an anchor) To lay out a second, smaller anchor to provide additional holding power.
(of a hunting dog) To stand still behind another dog which has pointed.
To push or force backwards.
definitions.example: The mugger backed her into a corner and demanded her wallet.

definitions.definition: To get upon the back of; to mount.
To place or seat upon the back.
To make a back for; to furnish with a back.
definitions.example: to back books

definitions.definition: To adjoin behind; to be at the back of.
To write upon the back of, possibly as an endorsement.
definitions.example: to back a letter;  to back a note or legal document

definitions.definition: (of a justice of the peace) To sign or endorse (a warrant, issued in another county, to apprehend an offender).
To row backward with (oars).
definitions.example: to back the oars

definitions.definition: (collective) The scientific and cultural community engaged in higher education and research, taken as a whole.
definitions.example: Academia continues to provide scientific education, despite attempts to turn it into a system of professional schooling.

definitions.definition: Continuous study at higher education institutions; scholarship.
definitions.example: Not every university graduate wishes to pursue academia.

definitions.definition: A peddler, huckster, who travels about to sell easily transportable goods.
Any dragonfly of the family Aeshnidae; a darner.
Someone who breeds and trains hawks and other falcons; a falconer.
The process of obscuring information to make it unreadable without special knowledge, key files, or passwords.
A ciphertext, a cryptogram, an encrypted value. Usually used with the preposition "of" followed by the value that is hidden in it.
An official face-to-face meeting of monarchs or other important figures.
Any face-to-face meeting, especially of an official nature.
A conversation in person (or, by extension, over the telephone, Internet etc.) between a journalist and someone whose opinion or statements he or she wishes to record for publication, broadcast etc.
definitions.example: The reporter gave the witness an interview.

definitions.definition: A formal meeting, in person, for the assessment of a candidate or applicant.
definitions.example: It was a dreadful interview; I have no hope of getting the job.

definitions.definition: An audition.
A police interrogation of a suspect or party in an investigation.
To ask questions of (somebody); to have an interview.
definitions.example: He interviewed the witness.

definitions.definition: To be interviewed; to attend an interview.
A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
A son of one's child.
An escape from prison.
To escape from prison.
To modify the firmware of an electronic device, especially a mobile phone, in order to remove restrictions that prevent it from running unofficial software.
Outside, externally. This is still used in the names of some civil parishes in England, e.g. St Cuthbert Without.
Lacking something.
definitions.example: Being from a large, poor family, he learned to live without.

definitions.definition: In prostitution: without a condom being worn.
Outside of, beyond.
definitions.example: The snow was swirling without the cottage, but it was warm within.

definitions.definition: Not having, containing, characteristic of, etc.
definitions.example: It was a mistake to leave my house without a coat.

definitions.definition: Not doing or not having done something.
definitions.example: He likes to eat everything without sharing.

definitions.definition: Unless, except (introducing a clause).
